headline: Does Allstate’s Q1 Turnaround And $4 Billion Buyback Change The Bull Case For ALL?
summary: Earlier this month, Allstate reported a sharp improvement in its Q1 2026 combined ratio to 82% and underwriting income to US$2.70 billion, while authorizing a new US$4.00 billion share repurchase program running through 2028 and continuing capital returns via an 8% dividend increase. Together with CEO-related stock option gifts and a Form 13F showing over US$9.35 billion in institutional holdings, these actions highlight management’s confidence in Allstate’s turnaround and capital strength...
